etymology.description: 抱(抱) (bào, "embrace") suggests holding onto something, often implying care or concern, while 歉(歉) (qiàn, "apologize") signifies a shortage or lacking, leading to the idea of not having fulfilled something. The combination of an embrace (implying sincerity) with an apology gives the sense of being "sorry" or offering "my apologies."
etymology.interpretation: 抱(抱) (bào, "embrace") represents holding onto something with care, showing a desire to make a situation right, combining with 歉(歉) (qiàn, "apologize") which indicates acknowledging a deficiency or failing. Together, they capture the idea of sincerely acknowledging a shortcoming or error and expressing regret, leading to the meaning of "sorry" or "my apologies."

examples.chinese: 我很抱歉(我很抱歉)，因为我迟到了(因為我遲到了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ hěn bàoqiàn, yīnwèi wǒ chídào le.
examples.english: I am very sorry, because I am late.

examples.chinese: 他感到抱歉(他感到抱歉)，不能参加聚会(不能參加聚會)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào bàoqiàn, bùnéng cānjiā jùhuì.
examples.english: He feels sorry that he cannot attend the party.

examples.chinese: 抱歉(抱歉)，这不是我故意的(這不是我故意的)。
examples.pinyin: Bàoqiàn, zhè bù shì wǒ gùyì de.
examples.english: Sorry, I didn't do this on purpose.
